calendar.matters:
Trustees Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Apr-20-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Bernhard, via ZOOM, counsel for Selby Moore III, Trustee; Vincent Yu, via ZOOM, Franchesca Callejo, via ZOOM, Co-Counsel for Adrianne Vincent, Interested Party; Carolyn Cain, via ZOOM, counsel for Grace Plimpton, FKA Grace Polly Vincent, Petitioner in related case.

At the outset, the court confirms with counsel that a mediation is scheduled for the beginning of May 2021 in the San Francisco and Contra Costa County matters. Mr. Bernhard confirms that the Temporary Restraining Order ("TRO") filed in the Contra Costa matter was vacated by that court in early 2021. However, thereafter, Grace M. Plimpton, filed a new application in the Contra Costa action seeking a new TRO. That application for a TRO has been continued until after the parties have completed the mediation set for May, 2021. Ms. Cain advises that a hearing on a preliminary injunction is currently set for the Contra Costa matter and asks this court to do the same. The court advises counsel that any discussions regarding a preliminary injunction in this matter will not be heard without a submission of a formal motion of which at this time, there is none. Regardless, the judge in the Contra Costa matter would like the parties to mediate prior to ruling on the petition for a preliminary injunction to which this court is in agreement with. Mr. Bernhard also reports to the court that the Trustee has demands from four beneficiaries and would like to meet his obligations by making the distributions to said beneficiaries and thus believes that distributions are appropriate and necessary. The court also questions counsel regarding whether there is any objection to the court granting the petition in this matter to which there are none.

The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn; Clerk: Robert Woods; Bailiff: Deputy Tauscher. Not Reported.
